channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Indigenous group seeks cultural land restitution in B.C. negotiations
item.description: The Osoyoos Indian Band is engaged in land negotiations with the British Columbia government to reclaim culturally significant sites and ancestral burial grounds. Chief Clarence Louie emphasized that ...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk shifted to hospital as hunger strike enters day 21; Delhi Police detain Abhijeet Dipke amid Jantar Mantar protest
item.description: Climate activist and educator Sonam Wangchuk was shifted to Safdarjung Hospital by the Delhi Police after his health reportedly deteriorated during his indefinite hunger strike, which entered its 21st...

item.title: Poland's Visa-Free List Includes Three African Nations
item.description: Poland has released an official list of countries whose citizens can enter the country without a visa. Only three African nations—Mauritius, Seychelles, and São Tomé and Príncipe—are included. These c...

item.title: Mandela Day Family Outings: Exploring South Africa's Historical Sites
item.description: This article highlights six significant historical sites across South Africa where families can engage in meaningful educational experiences during Mandela Day. The focus is on sites that shaped Nelso...

item.title: Mkapa’s Legacy Impacts National Health Initiatives
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing influence of former Tanzanian President Benjamin Mkapa's policies on the country's healthcare sector. Nchimbi, a key figure in health advocacy, highlights how Mkapa's...

item.title: Eurovision 2023: A Look at the Contest and Its Impact
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest 2023, a flagship event in the world of music and culture, drew international attention as nations showcased their artistic talents through song. Held in Turin, Italy, the c...

item.title: China Denies Involvement in Racist Video and Reiterates Opposition to South China Sea Arbitration
item.description: The article discusses China's official response to a video published by its state-run newspaper China Daily, which depicted Filipinos as monkeys. Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian clarifi...

item.title: Weekend Edition of BusinessDay: July 18, 2026 Highlights
item.description: The article provides a weekend edition summary of BusinessDay's coverage from July 18, 2026. It includes updates on various sectors such as business, economy, markets, and real estate. The content hig...

item.title: Australia lags behind Europe in addressing sexualised coverage of women athletes
item.description: European Athletics and the European Broadcasting Union have introduced guidelines to prevent the sexualisation of women athletes in broadcasting. These rules aim to eliminate voyeuristic camera angles...

item.title: Review of 'Elle' prequel to 'Legally Blonde': Nostalgia and narrative flaws
item.description: The 'Legally Blonde' prequel 'Elle' is a nostalgic journey through the 1990s, praised for its fashion, soundtrack, and Lexi Minetree's portrayal of a teenage Elle Woods. However, the series faces crit...

item.title: Delhi Police Shifts Activist Sonam Wangchuk to Hospital Amid Hunger Strike
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on an indefinite hunger strike for 21 days, was shifted to a hospital by Delhi Police on July 18, 2026, citing deteriorating health conditions. The Delhi High Cou...

item.title: Veteran Radio Presenters Gidi and Ghost Celebrate 18 Years of Morning Show Success
item.description: Veteran radio presenter Gidi Ogidi has celebrated his long-time co-host Jacob 'Ghost' Mulee's 61st birthday, marking 18 years of collaboration on their popular morning show, Gidi na Ghost Asubuhi. The...

item.title: Wife objects to medical treatment for Sonam Wangchuk without consent
item.description: The Delhi Police shifted activist Sonam Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital, prompting his wife Gitanjali Angmo to demand consent for any medical treatment. She stated that no procedures should be adminis...

item.title: Congress Faces Backroom Deal to Merge U.S. and Israeli Military Capabilities
item.description: The article details concerns raised by Dennis Kucinich about a covert provision in the National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) that could integrate U.S. and Israeli military technologies without pub...

item.title: Parents advised to safeguard children from abuse
item.description: The article highlights the importance of parents taking proactive measures to prevent child abuse. It emphasizes the need for heightened awareness and vigilance within families to ensure children's sa...

item.title: DSWD Launches Expanded UPLIFT Cash Aid Program for 7.5 Million Households
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) announced the start of the expanded United Package for Livelihoods, Industry, Food, and Transport (UPLIFT) cash aid distribution on Monday, targ...

item.title: Oyo teacher explains 56-day kidnapping ordeal, dispels staged claims
item.description: A rescued Oyo teacher, Zachery Olatunde, has shared the harrowing details of his 56-day captivity after armed men abducted 44 pupils and teachers from three schools in Oyo State. He revealed that kidn...

item.title: US launches sustained strikes against Iranian military targets amid escalating regional tensions
item.description: The article details a series of intensified military actions between the United States and Iran, with US forces conducting airstrikes on critical infrastructure in Iran. Key incidents include the dest...

item.title: Chicago White Sox defeat Toronto Blue Jays 12-4 in game
item.description: The Chicago White Sox secured a decisive 12-4 victory over the Toronto Blue Jays on Friday night, extending their winning streak to four games. Sam Antonacci contributed a two-run home run, while Brad...

item.title: Kenya's Forex Reserves Hit KSh 1.83 Trillion, Covering Six Months of Imports
item.description: Kenya's foreign exchange reserves increased to USD 14.169 billion (KSh 1.83 trillion) as of July 16, 2026, providing six months of import cover, according to the Central Bank of Kenya (CBK). This mark...
